heading: Electronic stamp requirements; recognition of electronic stamp
label: 718s Electronic stamp requirements; recognition of electronic stamp
notes: Amendments2023—Subsec. (b). , substituted “shall—” for “shall, during the effective period of the electronic stamp—” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 118–25, § 2(c)(1)Subsec. (c). , substituted “through the first June 30 that occurs after the date of issuance of the electronic stamp by the State” for “for a period agreed to by the State and the Secretary, which shall not exceed 45 days”.Pub. L. 118–25, § 2(c)(2)
amendments: Amendments2023—Subsec. (b). , substituted “shall—” for “shall, during the effective period of the electronic stamp—” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 118–25, § 2(c)(1)Subsec. (c). , substituted “through the first June 30 that occurs after the date of issuance of the electronic stamp by the State” for “for a period agreed to by the State and the Secretary, which shall not exceed 45 days”.Pub. L. 118–25, § 2(c)(2)

children.text: to have the same format as any other license, validation, or privilege the State issues under the automated licensing system of the State; and
to specify identifying features of the licensee that are adequate to enable Federal, State, and other law enforcement officers to identify the holder.
bestow upon the licensee the same privileges as are bestowed by an actual stamp;
be recognized nationally as a valid Federal migratory bird hunting and conservation stamp; and
authorize the licensee to hunt migratory waterfowl in any other State, in accordance with the laws of the other State governing that hunting.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: c Duration
children.text: An electronic stamp issued by a State shall be valid through the first June 30 that occurs after the date of issuance of the electronic stamp by the State.
